articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zimbabwe’s Growth Paradox: Strong Headline Numbers, Fragile Foundations, An Economic Analysis
more.description: Zimbabwe’s recent macroeconomic trajectory has surprised many observers. Several years ago, projections that the country could rank among the region’s fastest-growing economies were met with considerable scepticism, understandably so, given the country’s turbulent history of hyperinflation, currency collapse, and political instability. Yet the data has largely vindicated that outlook. By Brighton Musonza According to the [...] The post Zimbabwe’s Growth Paradox: Strong Headline Numbers, Fragile Foundations, An Economic Analysis appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Edgars reinvents itself as credit giant in Zimbabwe’s struggling retail sector
more.description: Edgars Zimbabwe shifts to a credit-driven retail model, boosting revenue through financial services, manufacturing and US dollar operations. The post Edgars reinvents itself as credit giant in Zimbabwe’s struggling retail sector appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Luke-ing the Beast in the Eye: Zimbabwe Independence Day — A wedding without a bride

more.title: ZRP Releases Names Of Nine Victims In Toyota Quantum Blast, Death Toll Revised Downwards
more.description: Police have released the names of nine of the twelve people killed when a Toyota Quantum exploded on 16 April 2026 at around 12.45 pm at the 27km peg along the Bulawayo–Beitbridge Road.

more.title: Did war veterans sacrifice everything only to be paupers in the country they liberated?

more.title: Zimbabwe Gets Its AI Moment: Econet’s New Unit Opens the Door to Enterprise-Grade Computing
more.description: For years, the conversation around artificial intelligence in Zimbabwe has been largely aspirational — a subject of policy documents, conference panels, and cautious optimism rather than deployable infrastructure. That changed this week. Econet Wireless, Zimbabwe’s largest telecommunications group, has formally launched Econet AI, a dedicated business unit designed to bring enterprise-grade artificial intelligence capabilities within [...] The post Zimbabwe Gets Its AI Moment: Econet’s New Unit Opens the Door to Enterprise-Grade Computing appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Madhuku and Biti Lawsuit Mystery: Why the High Court ‘Struck Off’ the Police Challenge and What Happens Next
more.description: HARARE — When legal heavyweights Professor Lovemore Madhuku and Tendai Biti joined forces to sue the police, Zimbabweans expected a landmark constitutional battle that would redefine the limits of state power. Instead, the High Court has struck the case off the “urgent roll,” leaving the applicants in a judicial limbo that many critics argue is [...] The post Madhuku and Biti Lawsuit Mystery: Why the High Court ‘Struck Off’ the Police Challenge and What Happens Next first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .
